title: tricholaena ruficoma
intro: Tricholaena ruficoma, also known as the red grass or red tuft grass, is a species of grass native to Australia, particularly in the eastern and central regions. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and has been used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Tricholaena ruficoma include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and protecting against oxidative stress. The herbal preparations of Tricholaena ruficoma include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and topical creams for therapeutic use.
